title: Employment boost at Glasgow Prestwick following e-commerce growth

sections.heading: What happened
sections.content: PIK says that 150 new jobs have been created at the airport, with this number forecasted to rise to 200 by the end of the summer.

sections.heading: Key details
sections.content: Employment boost at Glasgow Prestwick following e-commerce growth photo_camera L-R: Ian Forgie, chief executive, Glasgow Prestwick Airport and Alan Gemmell, Member of Parliament (MP) for Central Ayrshire, Scotland

sections.heading: Why it matters
sections.content: Glasgow Prestwick Airport (PIK) has reported a significant rise in job numbers following recent growth in e-commerce industry activity. Ian Forgie, chief executive at PIK, said: “This is a significant increase in headcount at the airport as we expand our services, and is great news for the Airport and the Ayrshire economy.
